caption: Brown floodwater fills a gas-station forecourt to knee height; one corner of the canopy has partially collapsed with exposed wiring arcing sparks down into the water, the pumps are partially submerged, and a man wades through the water away from the station toward higher ground.
annotator_notes: Image matches prompt. Cross-class mutual-hazard rule: wiring_1 (leaking) ↔ water_1 (hazardous_in_context) — arcing wiring electrifies the floodwater; the conductive water in turn expands the effective ground-fault surface back to the energized canopy hardware, multiplying short paths and arc points. Both directions emitted as worsens. The wiring's outgoing harm to the man flows through water_1 (electrified medium). Pump labeled pump_1 (schema: singular). Canopy_1 has partially collapsed with sagging; man is wading some distance from it but still within general fall radius — kept may_harm edge with note. Man actively escaping (at-risk Distress). EFFECT RELABEL (fluid -> already-hazardous target): water_1->pump_1 changed may_harm -> increases_risk_to per may_harm truth condition. GENERAL may_harm RULE applied (push_18 episode): canopy_1->pump_1 relabeled may_harm -> increases_risk_to (already-hazardous targets).
